summary: Stunning UltraMatrix capture of the legendary 7/12/90 RFK show, highlighted by a transcendent Dark Star and an electrifying Music Never Stopped.
review: The July‑12‑1990 RFK performance is hailed as one of the best shows of the ’90 tour, with Jerry’s soaring solos on “Feel Like a Stranger,” a monster “Bertha,” and a must‑listen “Dark Star” that culminates in an unforgettable “Music Never Stopped.”  The UltraMatrix (soundboard‑plus‑audience) source delivers crystal‑clear instrument separation while preserving the rain‑soaked crowd ambience, making the recording as vivid as the night itself.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are five‑star and focus on exceptional musical moments with no substantive complaints about sound quality; the blend of a superb performance and an excellent matrix recording warrants near‑perfect scoring.

recording_quality.technical_notes: UltraMatrix mix provides clear, balanced instrument separation with ambient crowd noise; reviewers note no major fidelity problems despite the rain‑storm setting.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows organically from a rain‑slick first set into a second‑set jam that weaves Foolish Heart into Dark Star and builds to a climactic, thunderous Music Never Stopped; the intensity is amplified by the stormy atmosphere and crowd energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for incendiary guitar runs on “Feel Like a Stranger,” a soaring solo on “Dark Star”/“Music Never Stopped,” and an iconic stage presence (leg kick, glowing aura).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for his thick, supportive bass that carries the weight during Bertha and other high‑energy moments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond B3 is highlighted as a pivotal force—both musically and vocally (ad‑libs) in the first set; this is his final 1990 tour run before a long hiatus.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as “Godzilla stadium shaking drum swirl” during the second‑set jam, emphasizing powerful rhythmic drive.
